# The Rise of Video in Podcasts

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/618ed4140aeae666976e4752/1739551393222-0WCY2CC6XSX59JQCB156/unsplash-image-iv5LNuiUyt8.jpg)

The podcast industry is undergoing a significant transformation as video becomes an increasingly dominant format. Publishers and platforms are investing in video podcasts to attract larger audiences, improve engagement, and tap into the lucrative online video advertising market.

YouTube has become the largest platform for podcast consumption, surpassing Apple Podcasts and Spotify. Over 400 million hours of podcast content are watched monthly on YouTube’s TV app, with 34% of podcast consumers using YouTube as their primary platform.

Meanwhile, Spotify is investing heavily in video podcasts to compete. With 250+ million users streaming video podcasts and over 300,000 video podcast shows, the platform is focusing on monetization and engagement. Spotify’s video podcast campaigns have led to a 55% increase in purchase intent, and features like Streaming Ad Insertion allow better audience targeting.

Both platforms are shaping the future of video-driven podcasting, with YouTube leading in reach and discoverability, while Spotify focuses on monetization and ad performance.

Several factors are driving the shift toward video in podcasting:

Audience Preferences: Many podcast listeners prefer a visual element, especially younger audiences. Platforms like YouTube and TikTok have conditioned users to engage with short-form and long-form video content.

Better Discovery & Engagement: Video content allows for higher engagement through comments, likes, and shares. It also makes podcast discovery easier, as social media platforms prioritise video over audio in their algorithms.

Advertising Revenue Potential: Video ads have higher CPMs than audio ads, making them more attractive to advertisers. Video allows for sponsored product placements, branded integrations, and dynamic advertising.

Omnichannel Promotion: Short video clips of podcast episodes can be shared across Instagram, TikTok, and X helping drive traffic back to full episodes.

Some of the popular Irish podcasts that already have video content are:  The 2 Johnnies, Nicky Byrne HQ and The Offload.

**What This Means for Irish Advertisers**

The shift to video is redefining podcasting. While YouTube dominates the space, Spotify and other platforms are quickly adapting. Podcasters who embrace video stand to benefit from increased visibility, engagement, and advertising revenue. However, balancing video integration while maintaining the core appeal of audio storytelling will be key to long-term success. From a media buying perspective this means that Irish advertisers shouldn’t view Podcasts as an audio only format and by expanding their targeting to both viewers and listeners, advertisers can more effectively reach their target audience.

**Source:**

--- SOURCE 2: The Rise of Video Podcasting: What Content Creators ... ---
URL: https://podcasthawk.com/the-rise-of-video-podcasting-what-content-creators-need-to-know/

SUMMARY:
Data shows that 84% of podcast consumers now prefer video versions, highlighting this growing trend. Shifts in Consumer Behavior and Platform

FULL CONTENT:
![Podcast Hawk](https://podcasthawk.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Artboard-1-copy-15-100x100.png)

# The Rise of Video Podcasting: What Content Creators Need to Know

![The Rise of Video Podcasting: What Content Creators Need to Know for 2025](https://podcasthawk.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/The-Rise-of-Video-Podcasting-What-Content-Creators-Need-to-Know-for-2025-915x523.jpg)

### **Sign up now to [Podcast Hawk](https://app.podcasthawk.com/register " Podcast Hawk") for a 7 DAY FREE TRIAL – to start getting on Podcasts today.**

Are traditional audio-only podcasts losing their edge? With platforms like **YouTube** and **Spotify** embracing video formats, the podcasting landscape is evolving rapidly. This shift isn’t just a trend—it’s a game-changer for creators looking to expand their reach and engage audiences in new ways.

Recent data shows that video podcasts are gaining traction, with higher retention rates and deeper engagement. In fact, over 64% of new [podcast](https://podcasthawk.com/what-does-a-good-podcast-media-kit-contain/) consumers prefer video-first content. This blend of audio and visual elements creates a richer experience, making it easier to connect with your audience on a personal level.

For creators, this means adapting to a new *format* that combines storytelling with visual appeal. Platforms are innovating to support this transition, offering tools to streamline production and distribution. If you’re ready to stay ahead in the industry, now is the time to explore the potential of video podcasts.

### Key Takeaways

## Understanding the Evolution of Podcasting and Video Integration

![video podcast](https://podcasthawk.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/video-podcast-1024x585.jpg "video podcast")

![video podcast](https://podcasthawk.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/video-podcast-1024x585.jpg "video podcast")

Podcasting has come a long way since its early days as an audio-only medium. What started as a simple way to share stories and ideas has grown into a dynamic **landscape** of multimedia experiences. Today, *video* integration is reshaping how audiences engage with their favorite shows.

### Historical Growth of Audio and Video Podcasts

In the early 2000s, **podcasting** was primarily an audio-driven format. Shows like “This American Life” paved the way for a new era of storytelling. Over time, advancements in technology allowed creators to experiment with *video*, adding a visual layer to their content.

Platforms like **YouTube** played a significant role in this shift. By 2020, *video podcasts* began gaining traction, offering a richer experience for **listeners**. Data shows that 84% of podcast consumers now prefer video versions, highlighting this growing **trend**.

### Shifts in Consumer Behavior and Platform Preferences

Audiences today crave more than just audio. They want to see the **host**, observe facial expressions, and enjoy high production quality. This shift in **preference** has led to a surge in *video* consumption on platforms like **YouTube** and specialized podcast apps.

Brands are also adapting their **marketing** strategies to include video content. According to recent reports, 75% of **listeners** trust brands endorsed by their favorite podcasters. This trend underscores the importance of visual context in building trust and engagement.

For **creators**, understanding these changes is crucial. By embracing *video*, you can expand your reach and connect with your audience on a deeper level. To stay ahead, explore [emerging podcasting trends](https://www.teleprompter.com/blog/emerging-podcasting-trends) and adapt your content strategy accordingly.

## Educational Content and AI Tools Shaping Podcast Production

![educational podcast](https://podcasthawk.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/educational-podcast-1024x585.jpg "educational podcast")

![educational podcast](https://podcasthawk.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/educational-podcast-1024x585.jpg "educational podcast")

Educational podcasts are redefining how audiences consume knowledge. Studies [show](https://podcasthawk.com/how-podcasters-can-use-appearing-on-podcasts-to-grow-their-show/) that 88% of listeners now prefer shows that offer fresh insights. This shift highlights a growing demand for content that blends learning with entertainment.

### Rise of Educational Podcast Genres

Podcasts like *The Tim Ferriss Show* and *Freakonomics Radio* have set the standard for educational content. These shows combine expert interviews with actionable insights, making complex topics accessible. Listeners appreciate the balance of information and engagement.

Educational genres are evolving beyond traditional boundaries. They now include storytelling, case studies, and interactive formats. This approach keeps audiences hooked while delivering valuable knowledge.

### Innovative AI-Powered Production Techniques

AI tools are transforming podcast production. Automated transcripts, sound quality enhancement, and editing software save time and improve output. Creators can focus on content creation while technology handles the technical details.

According to recent surveys, 75% of creators now use AI tools in their workflows. These innovations are not only efficient but also enhance the listener experience. For example, [AI-powered systems](https://adamfard.com/blog/ai-in-podcasting) can analyze audience preferences to tailor content.

However, integrating AI comes with challenges. Creators must ensure authenticity and avoid over-reliance on automation. Balancing technology with human creativity is key to success.

Educational content and AI tools offer a strategic advantage in a competitive market. By embracing these trends, you can elevate your podcast and connect with a knowledgeable audience.

## Short Clips and Social Media Strategies for Podcast Growth

![short podcast clips](https://podcasthawk.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/short-podcast-clips-1024x585.jpg "short podcast clips")

![short podcast clips](https://podcasthawk.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/short-podcast-clips-1024x585.jpg "short podcast clips")

Short-form video is revolutionizing how podcasters connect with their audience. Platforms like **TikTok**, **YouTube Shorts**, and **Instagram Reels** are driving this change. These tools allow creators to repurpose long-form content into bite-sized clips, making it easier to attract new listeners.

According to recent data, 90% of marketers report increased brand awareness through video clips. This trend highlights the power of short-form content in modern podcast marketing. By extracting engaging highlights from full-length episodes, you can maintain viewer interest and boost engagement.

### Leveraging TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and Instagram Reels

Social media platforms are essential for podcast growth. **TikTok** and **Instagram Reels** offer a way to reach younger audiences, while **YouTube Shorts** taps into a broader demographic. These platforms prioritize quick, dynamic content, making them ideal for sharing podcast highlights.

For example, many podcasters use these platforms to share key moments from their episodes. This strategy not only drives traffic but also encourages rapid content sharing. By leveraging these tools, you can amplify your reach and build a loyal audience.

### Enhancing Engagement with Video Highlights

Video highlights are a powerful way to keep listeners engaged. They provide a snapshot of your podcast’s best moments, enticing viewers to explore more. Platforms like **YouTube** make it easy to create and share these clips, ensuring your content reaches a wider audience.

According to [Sweet Fish Media](https://www.sweetfishmedia.com/blog/the-2025-state-of-video-podcasts), consistent video posting is crucial for audience growth. By focusing on visual storytelling, you can create a more immersive experience for your listeners. This approach aligns with the growing trend of quick, dynamic media consumption.

Podcasters who embrace short-form content are seeing significant results. As highlighted by [Podcast Hawk](https://podcasthawk.com/grow-your-community-through-guest-podcasting-in-2025/), leveraging social media is key to building a strong community. By integrating these strategies, you can stay ahead in the competitive podcasting landscape.

## Podcast Monetization and Sponsorship Trends in 2025

By 2025, podcasters will have unprecedented opportunities to monetize their content. The industry is projected to surpass $4 billion in ad revenue, driven by innovative sponsorship models and live events. Platforms like **Spotify** are leading the charge, offering advanced tools for creators to maximize their earnings.

### Ad Revenue and Sponsorship Opportunities

Ad revenue remains a cornerstone of podcast monetization. Research shows that 81% of listeners engage with podcast ads, significantly higher than TV commercials. This makes podcasts a lucrative channel for brands. *Spotify* has introduced unique programs to help creators secure sponsorships, ensuring steady income streams.

Independent podcasters are also finding success in niche markets. By targeting specific audiences, they can secure better deals and higher ad rates. This trend is reshaping the market, offering more opportunities for growth and profitability.

### Live Events and Premium Content Models

Live-stream events are becoming a game-changer for podcasters. These events not only generate revenue but also deepen audience engagement. For example, live Q&A sessions or exclusive interviews can attract premium ticket sales.

Premium subscription models are another emerging trend. Platforms like **Spotify** allow creators to offer exclusive content to subscribers, providing a steady income. This approach is particularly effective for podcasters with loyal fan bases.

“Podcast advertising is expected to capture $2.5 billion of the U.S. ad spend in 2024, reflecting a 19% year-over-year increase.”

Strategic partnerships are also reshaping the market. Collaborations with brands and other creators can amplify reach and revenue. By leveraging these opportunities, podcasters can stay ahead in a competitive landscape.

## Live Podcasting and In-Person Engagement Opportunities

Live podcasting is transforming how creators connect with their audience. This format allows hosts to interact directly with listeners, creating a more immersive experience. As the demand for personal connections grows, in-person events are becoming a cornerstone of podcast growth.

### Growth of Live Podcasting Events

Live podcasting events have seen significant growth in recent years. Surveys show that audiences are eager to engage directly with their favorite hosts. Events like *Podfest* have become hotspots for creators and listeners alike, offering unique opportunities for real-time feedback and interaction.

Statistics reveal that 61% of podcast listeners prefer attending live events. This trend highlights the value of face-to-face engagement. By hosting live recordings, podcasters can deepen their connection with their audience and create memorable experiences.

### Building Community Through Direct Interactions

Direct interactions are reshaping the podcasting experience. When listeners meet hosts in person, it fosters a sense of community and loyalty. This connection goes beyond the audio or video format, creating lasting relationships.

Podcasters are leveraging live events to strengthen their brand. Exclusive meetups and Q&A sessions allow hosts to address audience questions directly. This approach not only enhances engagement but also builds trust.

As the industry evolves, live podcasting offers long-term benefits. By embracing this format, creators can expand their reach and create a loyal fan base. The future of podcasting lies in these personal, in-person connections.

## The Rise of Video Podcasting: What Content Creators Need to Know for 2025

As we move into 2025, video podcasts are becoming a cornerstone of the podcasting **landscape**. Platforms like **YouTube** and **Spotify** are leading this shift, offering tools that simplify *content creation* and distribution. For creators, this means adapting to a format that combines storytelling with visual appeal.

### Video Podcasting Formats and Production Best Practices

Creating a successful video podcast starts with the right setup. Invest in quality lighting, microphones, and cameras to ensure professional **production**. A well-designed studio can significantly enhance your *episode* quality and audience retention.

Editing is equally important. Use tools like Adobe Premiere or Final Cut Pro to streamline the process. Focus on balancing audio and visual elements to cater to both traditional and modern listeners. Consistent branding across your **show** also helps build recognition and trust.

### Cross-Platform Dissemination and Engagement Strategies

To maximize reach, distribute your **video** podcasts across multiple platforms. **YouTube** is ideal for long-form content, while **TikTok** and Instagram Reels are perfect for short clips. Repurpose highlights from your *episodes* to attract new viewers and drive traffic to your full-length content.

Social media is a powerful tool for engagement. Share behind-the-scenes footage, teasers, and Q&A sessions to keep your audience hooked. Platforms like **YouTube** also offer analytics to track performance and refine your *strategy*.

Integrating these strategies can lead to significant **growth** and loyalty. For example, leveraging [podcast guesting strategies](https://podcasthawk.com/podcast-guesting-a-killer-marketing-strategy-for-2025/) can amplify your reach and establish your brand as a thought leader.

By embracing video and cross-platform strategies, you can stay ahead in the competitive podcasting **landscape**. Focus on quality, consistency, and audience engagement to build a loyal following and achieve long-term success.

## Conclusion

Podcasters are embracing new formats to stay relevant in a dynamic industry. From audio-only beginnings to video integration, the **podcasting** landscape has transformed. Platforms like YouTube and Spotify are driving this change, offering tools for seamless *content creation* and distribution.

Adopting video enhances **listener** engagement and retention. It also opens new **revenue** streams through ads and sponsorships. By leveraging live events and cross-platform strategies, creators can build a loyal **community** and strengthen their **brand**.

As we look ahead, staying competitive means embracing advanced techniques. Focus on quality production, consistent *format*, and audience interaction. Now is the time to adapt and thrive in this evolving **market**.

--- SOURCE 3: Nearly half of US listeners prefer video ---
URL: https://podspike.com/podcast-marketing-tips-and-advice/articles/nearly-half-of-us-podcast-listeners-prefer-video/12295/

SUMMARY:
Among Americans who had listened to a podcast in the past month, 46% said they preferred video, while 42% preferred audio-only. The preference for video skewed

FULL CONTENT:
![Podspike logo](https://podspike.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/06/Podspike-website-logo-20.07.21.png)

# Nearly half of US listeners prefer video

![Man wearing headphones and baseball cap focused on screen of a mobile phone. Credit: Attentie Attentie on Unsplash](https://podspike.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/attentie-attentie-CgKpB3iSX5c-unsplash-scaled-e1674330294691.jpg)

More podcast listeners in America prefer video with their shows than those who say they would rather go without, according to research conducted by Morning Consultant. 

Among Americans who had listened to a podcast in the past month, 46% said they preferred video, while 42% preferred audio-only. The preference for video skewed towards Gen Z and millennials. 

The most common reasons given by respondents was that they could see facial expressions and reactions, and that video helped them to focus on the podcast. 

[According to the survey](https://morningconsult.com/2023/01/11/podcasts-listen-watch-with-video/) conducted at the end of October, the preferred platform of podcast listeners was YouTube (33%), followed by Spotify (24%) and Apple (12%).

## Fewer new podcasts launched

The number of new podcasts launched has dramatically reduced over the past two years, according to a report from the Verge. 

Citing data drawn from the Listen Notes podcast search engine, the number of new podcasts in 2022 was about 219,000, down from a high of more than a million in 2020.

This, [as the article points out](https://www.theverge.com/2023/1/17/23559638/new-podcasts-down-apple-spotify-amazon-audible-hot-pod-summit), would have been influenced by the pandemic but is significantly lower than the numbers from 2019 (337k).

The [Listen Notes figures](https://www.listennotes.com/podcast-stats/) also reveal that while there have already been more than 7,000 podcasts launched in 2023, the total for the month is likely to fall well short of the 23,903 launched in January 2022.

## Spotify closing in on 5 million shows

Spotify has claimed to have 4.7 million podcast titles.

In a press release giving notice of its financial results for the final quarter of 2022 (which will be posted on January 31), the Swedish company describes itself as “the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 456 million Monthly Active Users”. 

In a similar press release on October 3, the number of podcast titles was given as 4.4 million. One year ago, the number given was 3.2 million.

SUMMARY:
Spotify's video podcast consumption grew 70% year-over-year in 2023. These statistics highlight the growing preference for video content, particularly among

FULL CONTENT:
![](https://www.facebook.com/tr?id=982818286913165&ev=PageView&noscript=1)
![GoReccie](https://goreccie.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/GORECCIE_F1.png "GoReccie - Book perfect location for your shoots")
![GoReccie](https://goreccie.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/GORECCIE_F1.png "GoReccie - Book perfect location for your shoots")

# Why is Video Podcasting the Future of the Industry?

![Why is Video Podcasting the Future of the Industry?](https://goreccie.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/unnamed-1.png)

Podcasting has long been an audio-first medium, but a major shift is underway—video podcasting is rapidly becoming the industry standard. With platforms like Spotify, YouTube, and Apple Podcasts investing heavily in video, a lot of creators are realizing the potential of visual storytelling to hold audiences interest and improve brand engagement at the same time.  

So, why is video podcasting gaining importance, and how can businesses and creators make use of this trend?

 

# **The Rise of Video Podcasting – What are the Numbers Saying?**

If we look at the data, it paints a clear picture—video podcasting is on the rise:  

These statistics highlight the growing preference for video content, particularly among younger audiences who are used to consuming media through platforms like YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok.  

**Video Content Improves Engagement and Retention**

One of the biggest advantages of video podcasting is higher engagement rates.  

**Video Podcasts Expand Monetization Opportunities**

When it comes to video, podcasters can make money beyond traditional audio ads with this type of content. Here is how:

YouTube Ad Revenue – Monetize your content through YouTube’s Partner Program.  

Sponsored Brand Integrations – Brands prefer video placements for higher engagement.  

Affiliate Marketing – Video product placements feel more authentic and drive more conversions.  

Exclusive Content & Memberships – Platforms like Patreon and YouTube Memberships allow creators to offer premium video content.  

Example: Logan Paul’s IMPAULSIVE Podcast  

Logan Paul transitioned from vlogging to video podcasting, creating one of the most-watched podcasts on YouTube. His sponsorship deals, YouTube ads, and brand partnerships generate millions in revenue.  

# **Why Are Creators Choosing YouTube for Podcasts?**

Better discoverability – YouTube’s search engine and recommendations help reach new listeners.  

Visual branding – Thumbnails, video intros, and dynamic backgrounds enhance recognition.  

Audience interaction – Viewers can comment, like, and engage in discussions.  

Example: The H3 Podcast  

Ethan Klein’s H3 Podcast thrives on YouTube because of its interactive visual elements, live-streamed discussions, and audience engagement in the comments section.  

# **Social Media and Video Podcasts Go Hand in Hand**

Short-form video content is dominating social media, and video podcasts fit seamlessly into this trend. So how does video podcasts improve social media reach?

Clips go viral – Short, engaging moments from video podcasts are perfect for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.  

Live streaming builds real-time engagement – Hosting live video podcasts allows for audience interaction via comments and Q&A sessions.  

Repurposing content saves time – One video podcast can be turned into multiple social media posts.    

# 

# **Video Podcasting Builds Stronger Personal Branding**

For entrepreneurs, industry leaders, and influencers, video podcasting is an authentic way to establish authority and build trust.    
  
Now, how does that happen?

-People connect with faces, not just voices.  

-Body language and expressions add authenticity.  

-Consistent branding (logo, colors, studio setup) enhances recognition.  

Example: Gary Vaynerchuk’s The GaryVee Audio Experience  

Gary Vee leverages video podcasting to reinforce his brand, repurposing long-form discussions into bite-sized content that aligns with his high-energy, motivational persona. 

# **The Future of Podcasting is Hybrid: Audio + Video**

While audio podcasts still have their place, the future is a hybrid approach where creators produce both audio and video content.  

What This Means for Podcasters? 

-Spotify and Apple Podcasts are integrating more video capabilities.  

-Brands and advertisers are shifting budgets towards video-based content.  

-Listeners now expect the option to watch OR listen to podcasts.  

According to Spotify’s CEO Daniel Ek, “*Video is the next frontier for podcasting. We’re seeing a clear trend where people engage longer when they have the option to watch and listen*.”  

# **Should You Start a Video Podcast?**

The video podcasting trend isn’t just a fad—it’s the future. Creators will see higher engagement and retention, more monetization opportunities, better discoverability on YouTube and social media, and stronger personal branding and audience connection.

How to [Get Started with Video Podcasting](https://goreccie.com/launch-your-podcast)? You can leave that on us! Get in touch with our team at GoReccie.

# Podcast Listening Hits New Highs As Video Changes The Game.

![Music fans - Getty Images](https://bloximages.newyork1.vip.townnews.com/insideradio.com/content/tncms/assets/v3/editorial/6/a5/6a58b05c-0425-4ab1-845c-4b6619f4e422/67fcad0663781.image.jpg)

Podcast consumption in the U.S. has reached unprecedented levels, and the rise of video is accelerating industry momentum, according to Edison Research’s newly released The Podcast Consumer 2025 report. The annual study offers a detailed look at the evolving behaviors, platforms and preferences of podcast audiences.

“It’s beyond doubt that in 2025 podcasts have achieved mainstream dominance,” Edison VP Megan Lazovick said Wednesday during a presentation of the new report. Backed by a decade of longitudinal data from The Infinite Dial, Share of Ear, Edison Podcast Metrics, and multiple custom studies, the report paints a picture of a thriving medium that has expanded far beyond its audio roots.

**Record-High Reach and Time Spent**

In 2025, an estimated 158 million Americans are monthly podcast consumers — up from 46 million in 2015. The growth in weekly listening is even more dramatic: 115 million weekly consumers now spend a total of 773 million hours per week with podcasts, a 355% increase over 2015.

“Since 2015, the number of people listening to podcasts has increased by millions, and we also know that those who do listen to podcasts are spending more time doing so,” Lazovick said. “So if you consider that — more people and more time — you see a massive increase in the total number of hours spent listening.”

The format now reaches 73% of Americans age 12 and older, up from 11% in 2006 and 50% in 2019. In 2025, 55% are monthly listeners and 40% are weekly consumers.

**The Rise of Video Podcasting**

One of the biggest shifts highlighted in the report is the rise of video podcasting. More than half (51%) of the U.S. 12+ population has watched a video podcast, 37% have done so in the past month, and 26% are weekly viewers.

“It feels as if some people are just now waking up to the opportunities in video podcasting,” Lazovick said. “But I can assure you that the audience has been awake, eyes wide open and watching for some time now.”

While most podcast consumers engage with both audio and video, video viewers stand out: they skew younger, are more racially diverse, and are more likely to use a range of devices and platforms — including computers, TVs, and smartphones — for podcast consumption.

Video podcast viewers also discover content differently. YouTube is the leading source of podcast discovery for Gen Z, followed closely by social media and friends or family. “Video consumers are increasingly flipping from one platform to another,” Lazovick noted.

**A Diverse and Valuable Audience**

The report highlights podcasting’s appeal across age, race and income levels. Among those age 12 to 34, 66% are monthly listeners. For ages 35 to 54, the figure is 61%, and for 55+ it’s 38%, each a record high.

Among Black Americans, 58% are monthly podcast consumers, up from 33% in 2020. Latino listeners also reached a record: 51% are now monthly consumers, more than double the 24% recorded five years ago.

Podcast audiences are also more affluent and educated than the general population. Forty-seven percent of monthly listeners earn more than $75,000 per year, compared to 43% of the U.S. 12+ population. Fifty-one percent have college degrees, and weekly podcast consumers are more likely to be business owners (18%) and homeowners (56%).

**Strong Ad Performance and Fandom**

Podcast listeners remain highly receptive to advertising. Edison’s data shows that 88% of weekly consumers agree that ads are a fair trade for free content, and 68% say they don’t mind hearing ads.

More importantly, advertising works: 44% of weekly podcast listeners age 13+ have purchased a product after hearing it promoted in a show, up from 34% in 2020. Lazovick attributes much of this to podcast fandom. Fans — defined as monthly listeners who score themselves 5 or higher on a 10-point engagement scale — spend over nine hours per week with podcasts, nearly four hours more than casual listeners.

Fandom translates into brand loyalty:

Among women, the numbers are even stronger: 75% of women 18+ who hear podcast ads say they’ve taken action as a result.

**Tech-Driven Growth**

Technology continues to shape how and where podcasts are consumed. Smart TVs, now in 75% of U.S. homes, are driving in-home podcast listening. Among those using internet-connected TVs, 42% of their listening time is spent with podcasts — more than music videos or streaming music.

In the car, the link between dashboard tech and podcast listening is clear. While 31% of Americans age 18+ say they’ve listened to a podcast in their primary vehicle, that jumps to 44% among those who use Apple CarPlay or Android Auto.

“The newer the vehicle, the more likely [drivers] are to report listening to podcasts in the car,” Lazovick said.

**A Competitive Advantage for Advertisers**

Lazovick said the data lays out a solid case for why “podcasts deserve brands’ budgets.” Not only do they have massive and growing reach with premium demographics, but she noted the medium has high engagement and expanding video reach.

“Podcasts offer a competitive advantage,” she said. “These are all the things you can tout when you are talking about the significance and value of podcasts and podcast consumers.”

Download Edison’s Podcast Consumer 2025 [HERE](https://www.edisonresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/The-Podcast-Consumer-2025-revised-FINAL.pdf).

enriched_points.expanded_content: Podcast audiences increasingly prefer video formats, with 46% favoring visual elements for engagement, particularly among younger demographics who thrive on platforms like YouTube.
Listeners seek more than passive consumption; they want engagement through comments, likes, and live interactions, enhancing their connection with hosts and content.

With the rise of algorithms, audiences now expect tailored content suggestions based on their preferences, making personalized recommendations crucial for retaining listeners in the podcasting landscape.
